summary: All steps completed in a single iteration. Fixed mixed fan-in early-cutoff bug in both edit_param (sequential, lib.rs) and ConcurrentEvalAdapter (concurrent_eval.rs). Sequential fix uses has_changed_parent HashSet seeded from changed param's dependents; concurrent fix uses SkipState struct behind a Mutex for atomic check-and-modify. Added 6 tests: 2 reproducing the bug (sequential + concurrent), 2 triple fan-in variants, 1 freshness postcondition check, 1 regression guard for valid early cutoff. All 161+ tests pass.

All 10 steps completed. Gap 1 (ConcurrentEditResult discards resolution metadata): added resolved_params and diagnostics fields to ConcurrentEditResult, wired resolve_concurrent_edit return values into the result. Gap 2 (no concurrent constraint checking): factored constraint logic into Engine::check_constraints_with_values helper, created edit_check_concurrent function combining concurrent edit + constraint checking. Gap 3 (stale comment): removed 'Currently FAILS' note. Added 5 new tests: resolved_params flow, infeasible diagnostics flow, constraint satisfaction, constraint transitions, and end-to-end resolution+constraints. All 260 tests pass.

description: Fixed clippy::let_and_return lint in SequencedMockConstraintSolver::solve() by returning the if-expression directly instead of via intermediate let binding.

summary: All 4 remaining steps completed. Gap 4 (constraint label regression): added label field to ConstraintNodeData, populated it from CompiledConstraint.label in the graph builder, updated check_constraints_with_values to use cnode.label.clone() instead of hardcoded None. Updated all ConstraintNodeData construction sites in graph.rs tests, demand.rs, and deps.rs. Added 2 tests: edit_check_preserves_constraint_labels (sequential path, initially failing) and edit_check_concurrent_preserves_constraint_labels (concurrent path, passes immediately). All 495 tests pass across the workspace.

All 3 prerequisites and 20 steps completed. Implemented: (1) reify-stdlib eval_builtin with 21 math functions (abs, sqrt, min, max, floor, ceil, round, sign, log, log10, exp, pow, sin, cos, tan, asin, acos, atan, atan2, sinh, cosh, tanh) with proper type dispatch for Int/Real/Scalar/Angle. (2) Wired reify-expr FunctionCall evaluation to dispatch through stdlib with strict Undef propagation. (3) Compiled import declarations into CompiledModule.imports with warning diagnostics. (4) Compiled sub-structure declarations into TopologyTemplate.sub_components with arg expression compilation via parent scope. (5) Added sub_component() and import() builder methods. Added 31+ new tests across stdlib, expr, and compiler. All workspace tests pass.

All 4 remaining steps completed (NaN/inf domain-error hardening). Step-21: wrote 7 failing tests for domain errors (sqrt(-1), log(0), log(-1), log10(0), log10(-1), exp(1000), pow(-2,0.5)) — all correctly failed showing NaN/inf being returned instead of Undef. Step-22: added sanitize_value helper that converts non-finite f64 in Value::Real and Value::Scalar to Undef, applied in unary_f64 and binary_f64 — all 7 tests now pass plus 22 existing tests. Step-23: wrote 8 more tests for asin/acos out-of-range, sinh/cosh overflow, and boundary valid inputs — all pass. Step-24: full workspace test suite passes with 0 failures. This completes all 24 steps of the plan.

All 4 steps completed. Step-1: wrote 4 failing tests for DimensionVector::root() (area→length, length^4→length^2, length→fractional, dimensionless→dimensionless). Step-2: implemented root(n) method that divides each Rational exponent by n via Rational::new(num, den*n). Step-3: wrote 4 failing tests for dimension-aware sqrt in stdlib (scalar area, scalar length^4, fractional exponent, negative scalar). Step-4: replaced sqrt from unary_f64 to unary with Scalar pattern match using dimension.root(2), fallback to as_f64 for Int/Real. All workspace tests pass with 0 failures.

All prerequisite and 6 steps completed. Pre-1: added #[doc(hidden)] poison helper methods to ConcurrentEvalAdapter (poison_skip_state, poison_results, poison_values, poison_snapshot_values) — used #[doc(hidden)] instead of #[cfg(test)] since integration tests need access. Steps 1-2: wrote 4 failing tests for accessor poison recovery (is_dirty, values, take_results, skipped), then fixed 4 .unwrap() calls to .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()). Steps 3-4: wrote failing test for evaluate() with poisoned values lock, then fixed 5 .unwrap() calls in evaluate(). Steps 5-6: wrote failing test for SharedPriorityPromoter with poisoned inner Mutex (tests all 6 operations), then fixed all 6 .unwrap() calls. All workspace tests pass with 0 failures. Total: 15 .unwrap() calls on lock acquisitions replaced with consistent PoisonError recovery pattern.

Completed steps 7-8 (post-review feature gating). Step-7: added test-utils feature to Cargo.toml, replaced #[doc(hidden)] with #[cfg(any(test, feature = "test-utils"))] on poison helper impl block, wrapped 5 integration tests in #[cfg(feature = "test-utils")] module. Step-8: verified (1) cargo check without feature compiles (poison methods excluded from production API), (2) cargo test --features test-utils passes all 23 integration tests + 73 unit tests, (3) cargo test without feature passes 18 integration tests + 73 unit tests (poison tests correctly excluded). This completes all 8 steps of the plan.

All 1 prerequisite and 8 steps completed. Fixed OCCT kernel unwrap panics on null shape handles: (1) Added #[cfg(test)] insert_null_shape helper using cxx::UniquePtr::null(). (2) Fixed get_shape() two-step error handling: missing key → InvalidReference, null ptr → OperationFailed. (3) Fixed warm_state() with let-else guard to skip null shapes. (4) Added parameter validation (> 0) for Box/Cylinder/Sphere/Fillet dimensions before FFI calls. (5) Added integration test proving kernel thread survives multiple error types. Total: 48 tests pass (9 new), 0 failures.

Completed final 2 steps (NaN/infinity parameter validation). Step-9: wrote 9 failing tests for NaN, +infinity, -infinity across Box/Cylinder/Sphere/Fillet — 6 correctly failed (NaN and +inf slip through x <= 0.0), 3 already passed (-inf caught by <= 0.0, fillet NaN/inf caught by OCCT FFI). Step-10: replaced all x <= 0.0 guards with \!(x.is_finite() && x > 0.0) — all 57 tests pass (9 new + 48 existing). This completes all 10 steps of the plan.

All 2 prerequisites and 8 steps completed. Pre-1: added minimize_declaration and maximize_declaration rules to grammar.js following constraint_declaration pattern. Pre-2: regenerated tree-sitter parser. Step-1: wrote 2 failing parse tests for minimize/maximize. Step-2: added MinimizeDecl/MaximizeDecl AST types, MemberDecl variants, lower_minimize()/lower_maximize() methods, updated all exhaustive matches. Step-3: added 3 parse validation tests (complex expr, mixed members, span/hash). Step-4: wrote failing compile test for objective field. Step-5: added objective field to TopologyTemplate, compiled minimize/maximize into OptimizationObjective, added builder method. Step-6: added maximize compile test and backward compatibility test. Step-7: added E2E round-trip integration test. Step-8: fixed boundary1_producer.rs exhaustive match, all 618 workspace tests pass with 0 regressions.

Completed pre-1 through pre-4 (all prerequisites, most already done from prior iterations). Steps 1-8 done: per-declaration where clause compilation, block guard compilation with nested guard conjunction, and else block compilation. Steps 9,11: reference safety tests written (unguarded-to-guarded fails as expected, same-guard passes). Step 10 in progress: extended CompilationScope with guard tracking, added compile_expr_guarded with reference safety checking, but auto-linter introduced duplicate Conditional match arm bug in check_expr_references that needs fixing before tests pass. Steps 12-20 still pending.

Add CompiledGuardedGroup struct and extend TopologyTemplate with guarded_groups: Vec<CompiledGuardedGroup> and structure_controlling: HashSet<ValueCellId> fields in reify-compiler/src/lib.rs. Add 'use std::collections::HashSet' import. Update compile_structure() to initialize empty guarded_groups/structure_controlling. Update content_hash computation to include guarded group hashes. Update TopologyTemplateBuilder.build() in reify-test-support/src/builders.rs to initialize new fields (empty defaults). Ensure all existing tests still compile.; Add guarded_group() builder method to TopologyTemplateBuilder in reify-test-support/src/builders.rs. Method accepts guard_expr (CompiledExpr), guard_value_cell (ValueCellId), members (Vec<ValueCellDecl>), constraints (Vec<CompiledConstraint>), else_members (Vec<ValueCellDecl>), else_constraints (Vec<CompiledConstraint>) and appends to internal guarded_groups vec and structure_controlling set. Update build() content_hash to include guarded groups.; Add GuardedGroupInfo struct and guarded_groups: Vec<GuardedGroupInfo>, structure_controlling: HashSet<ValueCellId> fields to EvaluationGraph in reify-eval/src/graph.rs. GuardedGroupInfo holds: guard_cell (ValueCellId), members (Vec<ValueCellId>), constraints (Vec<ConstraintNodeId>), else_members (Vec<ValueCellId>), else_constraints (Vec<ConstraintNodeId>). Update topology_fingerprint() to include guard-related hashes. Initialize fields empty in Default.; Create empty test files: crates/reify-compiler/tests/guard_compilation.rs and crates/reify-eval/tests/guard_eval.rs with basic imports from reify_compiler, reify_test_support, reify_types, reify_eval. Verify cargo test --no-run succeeds.; Write failing test in guard_compilation.rs: compile_param_with_where_clause. Parse `structure S { param active : Bool = true
 param x : Scalar = 5mm where active }` via reify_syntax::parse then reify_compiler::compile. Assert TopologyTemplate.guarded_groups has 1 entry with guard_expr referencing 'active', members contains one ValueCellDecl for x, and guard_value_cell is in structure_controlling. Assert x is NOT in the top-level value_cells.; Implement per-declaration guard compilation in compile_structure(). In both passes: check where_clause on ParamDecl/LetDecl/ConstraintDecl. In pass 1, still register guarded names in scope (so they can be referenced). In pass 2: (1) compile guard condition via compile_expr, (2) create synthetic guard ValueCellId (entity, '__guard_N'), (3) create guard ValueCellDecl (Bool, Let, default_expr=guard_condition), (4) create CompiledGuardedGroup with the member, (5) do NOT add guarded member to top-level value_cells, (6) add guard_value_cell to structure_controlling. Use a guard_index counter for unique naming.; Write failing test: compile_block_guard. Parse `structure S { param active : Bool = true
 where active { param x : Scalar = 5mm
 param y : Scalar = 10mm
 constraint x > 2mm } }`. Assert guarded_groups has 1 entry with 2 members (x, y ValueCellDecls) and 1 constraint under the same guard.; Implement block guard compilation. Replace the no-op GuardedGroup(_) arm in compile_structure(). When encountering MemberDecl::GuardedGroup(g): (1) compile g.condition, (2) create synthetic guard ValueCell, (3) iterate g.members compiling each Param/Let into CompiledGuardedGroup.members and Constraint into CompiledGuardedGroup.constraints, (4) add guard_value_cell to structure_controlling. Register block-guarded names in pass 1 as well.; Write failing test: compile_nested_guards. Parse `structure S { param a : Bool = true
 param b : Bool = true
 where a { where b { param x : Scalar = 1mm } } }`. Assert the inner guarded group's guard_expr is a BinOp::And referencing the outer guard ValueCell and inner condition 'b'. Assert 2 entries in guarded_groups (one per nesting level) or 1 entry with conjunctive guard.; Implement nested guard conjunction. Refactor guard compilation into a helper function that accepts Option<ValueCellId> for outer_guard. When outer_guard is Some, the inner guard_expr = CompiledExpr::binop(BinOp::And, ValueRef(outer_guard), inner_condition, Type::Bool). When compiling nested GuardedGroupDecl members, pass the current guard ValueCellId as outer_guard context.; Write failing test: compile_else_block. Parse `structure S { param cond : Bool = true
 where cond { param a : Scalar = 1mm } else { param b : Scalar = 2mm } }`. Assert guarded_groups[0] has members=[a ValueCellDecl] and else_members=[b ValueCellDecl], sharing the same guard_value_cell.; Implement else block compilation. When GuardedGroupDecl has non-empty else_members, compile each into CompiledGuardedGroup.else_members and else_constraints. Register else-block names in pass 1 for scope resolution. Else members are active when guard=false -- the eval engine handles conditional logic.; Write failing test: reference_safety_unguarded_to_guarded_error. Parse `structure S { param active : Bool = true
 param x : Scalar = 5mm where active
 let y = x }`. Unguarded let y references guarded x. Compile should produce a diagnostic error containing 'unguarded reference' or 'guarded'.

 let y = x }`. Unguarded let y references guarded x. Compile should produce a diagnostic error containing 'unguarded reference' or 'guarded'.; Implement reference safety checking. Extend CompilationScope.names to map name -> (ValueCellId, Type, Option<ValueCellId>) where the third element is the guard cell ID. Update register() to accept optional guard_cell. In compile_expr() for Ident, when resolved cell has a guard and current compilation context has no guard or weaker guard, push Diagnostic::error about unsafe reference. Pass guard context through compile of guarded group members.; Write test: reference_safety_same_guard_ok. Parse `structure S { param active : Bool = true
 where active { param x : Scalar = 5mm
 let y = x } }`. Both x and y are under the same guard 'active'. Compile should produce no reference safety error diagnostics.; Ensure same-guard and stronger-guard references pass validation. When both referencing context and referenced cell share the same guard_value_cell ID, no error is emitted. For conjunction guards (step-6), check if the referenced cell's guard ID appears in the referencing context's guard chain. This may already work from step-10; verify and fix edge cases.

Completed all 4 prerequisites and compiler-side steps 1-12. Pre-3/Pre-4 were already done from prior iterations. Steps 1-2: per-declaration where clause compilation with compile_per_decl_guard helper. Steps 3-4: block guard compilation with compile_block_guard and compile_guarded_members helpers, including nested guard support via recursive dispatch. Steps 5-6: nested guard conjunction (AND) already handled by compile_block_guard outer_guard parameter. Steps 7-8: else block compilation in compile_guarded_members. Steps 9-12: reference safety checking via post-compilation check_expr_references pass that walks compiled expressions and detects unguarded references to guarded cells. Same-guard references are safe. 6 guard compilation tests pass. NOTE: There is a remaining &scope vs &mut scope mismatch at line 907 for the top-level compile_block_guard call that needs fixing before eval-side work (steps 13-20) can begin. Eval-side steps 13-20 remain pending.
